messages.text:
OFFER: price=60
I am offering one unit of X for 60 ZUP.

RAW_UPSTREAM_RESPONSE:
<proposal count>1</proposal count>
<my resources>X: 1</my resources>
<my goals>Sell X for at least 40 ZUP.</my goals>
<reason>I am the seller and my cost is 40 ZUP, so I should start above cost to leave room for negotiation while aiming for the highest acceptable price. Since there is only one item, a clean opening offer is appropriate.</reason>
<player answer>PROPOSAL</player answer>
<newly proposed trade>Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60</newly proposed trade>
<message>I am offering one unit of X for 60 ZUP.</message>

messages.text:
OFFER: price=40
I can do 40 ZUP for 1 unit of X. That is a fair counteroffer and still within a workable range for both sides.

RAW_UPSTREAM_RESPONSE:
<proposal count>1</proposal count>
<my resources>ZUP: 1000</my resources>
<my goals>Buy resources with ZUP. You are willing to pay at most X: 100 ZUP for the resources.</my goals>
<reason>The seller’s offer of 60 is below my maximum value of 100, so a deal is possible. To improve my outcome, I should counter with a lower price while staying realistic. I will propose 40 as a reasonable midpoint and keep the negotiation open.</reason>
<player answer>PROPOSAL</player answer>
<newly proposed trade>Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40</newly proposed trade>
<message>I can do 40 ZUP for 1 unit of X. That is a fair counteroffer and still within a workable range for both sides.</message>

messages.text:
ACCEPT: price=40
Accepted at 40 ZUP for 1 unit of X.

<my goals>Sell X for at least 40 ZUP.</my goals>
